title.en: We help LGBTQI+ people facing persecution based on their sexual orientation, gender identity and sex characteristics.

children.text: As a result of Rainbow Railroad, more LGBTQI+ individuals can access lives free from persecution, and ultimately, we envision a world where LGBTQI+ people can live lives of their choosing.

label.en: Volunteer and become part of our mission

children.text: Our mission is to provide a critical lifeline to LGBTQI+ individuals worldwide who face persecution and violence simply for being who they are. We offer comprehensive support—including facilitating emergency travel, arranging temporary safe housing, and assisting with the complex process of finding safety—giving them a chance to live freely and rebuild their lives with hope and dignity.

title.en: Confronting the Double Bind of Anti-LGBTQI+ and Anti-Migrant Discrimination

title.en: Insights from our Work: LGBTQI+ Resettlement in Canada Demonstrates the Success of State-Civil Society Partnership

children.text: The Government-Assisted Refugee (GAR) program is a Canadian resettlement pathway that offers refugees a lifeline to resettle in Canada.

Rainbow Railroad was founded in 2006 as a volunteer-run organization by a diverse group of LGBTQI+ activists and human rights defenders who wanted to do more to address the levels of violence LGBTQI+ people face worldwide. Our name harkens back to the Underground Railroad – a network of activists in the 19th century, who assisted Black folks escape enslavement in the American South.
Rainbow Railroad scaled up its operations after receiving charitable status in Canada and the United States in 2013 and 2015 respectively.

There are currently 120 million displaced people around the world – the highest number since the end of the Second World War. We received over 15,000 requests for help in 2023 from LGBTQI+ people whose lives are in danger. While we cannot relocate everyone who reaches out to us, we offer a variety of programs to support individuals’ unique needs and circumstances. Working with partners on the ground, we get at-risk LGBTQI+ people to safety, worldwide.

We work in all regions around the world, based in Canada and the United States. Our work is completed in partnership with a global network of activists and human rights defenders advocating for LGBTQI+ rights in countries around the world. The people we help are often experiencing police harassment, family violence, employment discrimination or forced conversion therapy. In some cases, they endure life-threatening attacks perpetrated by members of their families or communities. This year, we’ll resettle people from over 40 countries into over 20 different countries of safety around the world.

We work in all regions around the world, based in Canada and the United States. Our work is completed in partnership with a global network of activists and human rights defenders advocating for LGBTQI+ rights in countries around the world. The people we help are often experiencing police harassment, family violence, employment discrimination or forced conversion therapy. In some cases, they endure life-threatening attacks perpetrated by members of their families or communities. This year, we’ll resettle people from over 40 countries into over 20 different countries of safety around the world.
Learn more about where we work and what we’ve done so far by reading our annual reports, found at the bottom of this page.

title.en: Your life is precious, your safety is important.

children.text: If you identify as LGBTQI+ and need support to make your life safer, we are here to help.
First, we need you to fill out our help form with
as many details as possible.

Everyone's route to safety is unique; we have multiple support programs that may fit your needs. The more details you are able to share with us the better. We know this is highly sensitive information so we keep
everything you share with us entirely confidential.
The next step involves sharing your story and experiences. While we know this can be overwhelming, sharing details and supporting documents about your situation will help us verify you.
We will provide you with a list of documents that can be used to validate your claim.

Once we verify your case, we will try to work with you to develop a solution to make your life safer. We attempt to offer emergency travel support when possible but we also have a number of other programs that focus on financial aid, applications for government sponsorship, in-country relocation or referrals to others who may be able to help.

subtitle.en: Fill out the form below to start the process. Read the form carefully and make sure to fill out the fields exactly as required or you will slow down the process for your assistance.
